description_long: This is a shallow, wide-mouthed bowl with a roughly circular rim and a visibly uneven, hand-crafted appearance. The exterior surface is a warm, earthy terracotta color, with subtle variations in tone and some lighter, almost cream-colored patches along the rim. The rim itself is thick and slightly irregular, featuring a faint, incised decorative band and a raised circular motif on one side. The bowl's interior is smoother but shows discoloration and wear, with some darker stains and patches. The overall structure appears fragile, with visible cracks, chips, and missing sections, especially along the rim and lower body.
description: A wide, shallow terracotta bowl with an uneven rim, incised band, and visible chips and cracks. The surface is earthy with lighter patches and signs of wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bowl's circular shape, thick rim with a raised motif, and the most intact section of the rim.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the bowl's uneven rim, visible cracks, and a large missing section on the lower right.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals extensive chipping and a darker, more worn interior surface, with the rim appearing most damaged.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizes the bowl's shallow depth, irregular rim, and a large break on the left side.
attribute_location: outdoor archaeological site or historical display
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, possibly for communal or ceremonial use
attribute_decorative_patterns: incised band and raised circular motif on the rim
